rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Modify; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ryan Lewis; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 15, 2025, Line 1. 1 - CROSS DEFENDANTs THE BANCORP BANK,and INCOMM FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. Motion To Modify; Request For Judicial Notice. Petitioner ITC Financial Licenses, Inc.'s ("ITC") motion to modify judgment is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Petitioner must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMAL TARGA VS. TOMMY BAILEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Targa And Request For Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Declaration Of Aurash Gomroki
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 15, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT TOMMY BAILEY's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Targa And Request For Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Tommy Bailey's unopposed Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Targa And Request For Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.450(a), which provides, "If, after service of a deposition notice, a party to the action or an officer, director, managing agent, or employee of a party, or a person designated by an organization that is a party under Section 2025.230, without having served a valid objection under Section 2025.410, fails to appear for examination, or to proceed with it, or to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice, the party giving the notice may move for an order compelling the deponent's attendance and testimony, and the production for inspection of any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice." Defendant satisfied their meet and confer obligations. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff Jamal Targa must appear to give testimony at deposition and produce for inspection the materials described in the deposition notice. The deposition shall take place in the manner and means stated in the notice and at a time and place agreeable by the parties but no later than December 29, 2025. If the parties cannot agree on a time or date, the deposition shall be on December 23, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. Plaintiff did not demonstrate the merit of any cognizable objection. Plaintiff Jamal Targa has not demonstrated he acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiff Jamal Targa shall pay $1,035 to Defendant Tommy Bailey as sanctions, payment no later than January 12, 2026. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010, 2023.030, 2025.450(g)(1).) To the extent a party seeks different or additional relief, those requests are denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MASOOD SAMEREIE VS. 15/40 PRODUCTIONS LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 15, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT WARNER BROS. DISTRIBUTING INC.'s Motion To File Cross-Complaint. Defendant Warner Bros. Distributing, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.) Good cause appearing, the court grants Defendant Warner Bros. Distributing, Inc. leave to file the proposed cross-complaint. Defendant Warner Bros. Distributing, Inc. must file its cross-complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit A to the Khoder Declaration, within seven calendar days of entry of this order. Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: MURIELLE MUTZEL VS. PEET'S COFFEE AND TEA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To (1) Compel Responses To Requests For Production; And (2) For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel, Saunders & Associates, Apc
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 15, 2025, Line 5. Defendant Peet's Coffee, Inc.'s 's Motion To (1) Compel Responses To Requests For Production; And (2) For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.310. This section applies only when further responses are sought. The relevant statute here is section 2031.300 The court finds the motion was timely filed and served. There is no contention that Defendant's RFPs addressed to Plaintiff Murielle Mutzel are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has not established that she provided any responses to the RFPs prior to this motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.300.) Plaintiff's failure to timely provide responses is not excused. There is no duty to meet and confer prior to making a motion such as this. In any event, Defendant has demonstrated meet and confer efforts. Plaintiff Murielle Mutzel shall serve verified, objection-free responses no later than December 30, 2025. Defendant's request for an order that Plaintiff produce all responsive documents by the same date is denied. Defendant did not cite section 2031.320, let alone demonstrate a basis for relief under it. In any event, currently the court sees no substantial basis for relief under section 2031,320. The court does not find Plaintiff Murielle Mutzel and her counsel acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiff Murielle Mutzel and her counsel Gary S. Saunders and Saunders & Associates, APC, jointly and severally, shall pay to Defendant, through counsel, $960 as sanctions, payment no later than January 6, 2026. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010, 2023.030, 2031.300(c).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NA VS. JAMES STEWART
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 15, 2025, Line 7. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NA Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s unopposed Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted is GRANTED. The record demonstrates Plaintiff properly served Requests for Admission and Defendant James Stewart did not provide any responses to Plaintiff's requests. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280.) Defendant's failure to timely respond is not excused. No meet and confer requirement when no responses have been provided. Plaintiffs, however, conferred. Plaintiffs' RFAs, Set One, dated August 8, 2025, are deemed admitted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: WOUTER WITVOET VS. ARC GLOBAL, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 15, 2025, Line 9. 2 - PLAINTIFF WOUTER WITVOET MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Plaintiff Wouter Witvoet's unopposed Motion to Admit Pro Hac Vice Counsel Eric S. Rosen is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. An application to appear as counsel pro hac vice "must state[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (California Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) The instant application contains none of this information. It is, therefore, denied without prejudice. Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
